title: Is SoundHound AI Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Continues to Soar?
text: SoundHound AI stock analysis recommends investors stay on the sidelines despite impressive growth metrics. SoundHound demonstrated strong financial momentum in Q1 2026 with overall revenue increasing 52% year-over-year to $44.2 million and organic automotive and IoT revenue surging 88%. Full-year 2026 guidance is $225-260 million, with projected 2027 combined revenue (post-LivePerson) of $350-400 million. However, the company remains unprofitable with adjusted EBITDA losses of $26.7 million, worsening from $22.2 million the prior year. Gross margins declined significantly—GAAP margins fell from 36.5% to 31.1% year-over-year due to vendor true-up costs. Operating cash outflow reached $26.3 million in the quarter despite holding $216 million in cash reserves. The LivePerson acquisition represents a major integration challenge, with the analyst noting the company is juggling a lot while attempting margin improvements and platform modernization simultaneously.
